I agree with Option A — tapered, screwed-in posts — being the optimal choice for mandibular molars. Their ability to conform closely to irregular root canal shapes enhances retention while distributing occlusal forces evenly throughout the root, minimizing localized stress concentrations that could otherwise initiate fractures. Additionally, the use of screws provides secure fixation without relying solely on cementation, offering predictable performance even if there’s minor variation in canal morphology or over time.

Final answer: A
